Add a new row of data to a specific spre: Add a new row of data to a specific spreadsheet.
Inserts a new row at the top of a worksh: Inserts a new row at the top of a worksheet, just below the header row.
Add multiple rows of data at once to a s: Add multiple rows of data at once to a specific spreadsheet.
Update the data in an existing row.
Updates multiple rows in a specific spre: Updates multiple rows in a specific spreadsheet.
Delete a specific row from the selected: Delete a specific row from the selected sheet.
Deletes a contiguous range of rows, or a: Deletes a contiguous range of rows, or a list of specific row numbers. Row numbers are 1-based.
Look up rows in a worksheet based on a c: Look up rows in a worksheet based on a column value.
Look up a row by column value; if no mat: Look up a row by column value; if no match is found, create a new row with the provided values.
Create a new blank worksheet with a titl: Create a new blank worksheet with a title.
Look up a worksheet by title in a spread: Look up a worksheet by title in a spreadsheet; if not found, create it with optional headers.
Clears all rows on an existing sheet.
Clears the contents of one or more rows: Clears the contents of one or more rows without removing the rows themselves. Useful when you want to keep formatting and references stable.
Format one or multiple rows in specific: Format one or multiple rows in specific spreadsheet.
Retrieve a specific row using its unique: Retrieve a specific row using its unique ID.
Get next group of rows from a specifiec: Get next group of rows from a specifiec workheet
Get all the rows from a specific sheet.
Read cells from a range using A1 notatio: Read cells from a range using A1 notation (e.g. A1:D10). Returns rows and the resolved range.
Creates a new worksheet by copying an ex: Creates a new worksheet by copying an existing one.
Creates a new column in a specific sprea: Creates a new column in a specific spreadsheet.
Download a worksheet as a CSV or TSV fil: Download a worksheet as a CSV or TSV file.
Make a custom API call to a specific end: Make a custom API call to a specific endpoint

Select the format of the input values to: Select the format of the input values to be added into the worksheet.

\n**Notes:**\n\n- Memory key is used to:
**Notes:**

- Memory key is used to remember where last row was processed and will be used in the following runs.
- Republishing the flow **keeps** the memory key value, If you want to start over **change** the memory key.
The key used to store the current row nu: The key used to store the current row number in memory
The number of rows to get: The number of rows to get
The cell range to read, e.g. A1:D10. Lea: The cell range to read, e.g. A1:D10. Leave empty to read the entire worksheet.
Whether to return rows (default) or colu: Whether to return rows (default) or columns. "ROWS" returns one array per row; "COLUMNS" returns one array per column.
How values should be represented in the: How values should be represented in the output.
Enter the name of the spreadsheet to sea: Enter the name of the spreadsheet to search for
If true, only return spreadsheets that e: If true, only return spreadsheets that exactly match the name. If false, return spreadsheets that contain the name.
If true, only return worksheets that exa: If true, only return worksheets that exactly match the name. If false, return worksheets that contain the name.
The column index starts from 1.For examp: The column index starts from 1.For example, if you want to add a column to the third column, enter 3.Ff the input is less than 1 the column will be added after the last current column.
